info.title: OpenSandbox Lifecycle API
info.description: The Sandbox Lifecycle API coordinates how untrusted workloads are created, executed, paused, resumed, and finally disposed.

post.description:
Create a sandbox from a container image.

Creates a new sandbox from a container image with optional resource limits,
environment variables, and metadata. Sandboxes are provisioned directly from
the specified image without requiring a pre-created template.

Args:
    request: Sandbox creation request
    x_request_id: Unique request identifier for tracing (optional; server generates if omitted).

Returns:
    CreateSandboxResponse: Accepted sandbox creation request

Raises:
    HTTPException: If sandbox creation scheduling fails

202.description: Sandbox creation accepted for asynchronous provisioning

400.description: The request was invalid or malformed

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

409.description: The operation conflicts with the current state

get.description:
List sandboxes with optional filtering and pagination.

List all sandboxes with optional filtering and pagination using query parameters.
All filter conditions use AND logic. Multiple `state` parameters use OR logic within states.

Args:
    state: Filter by lifecycle state.
    metadata: Arbitrary metadata key-value pairs for filtering.
    page: Page number for pagination.
    page_size: Number of items per page.
    x_request_id: Unique request identifier for tracing (optional; server generates if omitted).

Returns:
    ListSandboxesResponse: Paginated list of sandboxes

parameters.name: state

schema.description: Filter by lifecycle state. Pass multiple times for OR logic.
schema.title: State

parameters.description: Filter by lifecycle state. Pass multiple times for OR logic.

parameters.name: metadata

schema.description: Arbitrary metadata key-value pairs for filtering (URL encoded).
schema.title: Metadata

parameters.description: Arbitrary metadata key-value pairs for filtering (URL encoded).

400.description: The request was invalid or malformed

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

get.description:
Fetch a sandbox by id.

Returns the complete sandbox information including image specification,
status, metadata, and timestamps.

Args:
    sandbox_id: Unique sandbox identifier
    x_request_id: Unique request identifier for tracing (optional; server generates if omitted).

Returns:
    Sandbox: Complete sandbox information

Raises:
    HTTPException: If sandbox not found or access denied

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

403.description: The authenticated user lacks permission for this operation

404.description: The requested resource does not exist

delete.description:
Delete a sandbox.

Terminates sandbox execution. The sandbox will transition through Stopping state to Terminated.

Args:
    sandbox_id: Unique sandbox identifier
    x_request_id: Unique request identifier for tracing (optional; server generates if omitted).

Returns:
    Response: 204 No Content

Raises:
    HTTPException: If sandbox not found or deletion fails

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

403.description: The authenticated user lacks permission for this operation

404.description: The requested resource does not exist

409.description: The operation conflicts with the current state

post.description:
Pause execution while retaining state.

Pauses a running sandbox while preserving its state.
Poll GET /sandboxes/{sandboxId} to track state transition to Paused.

Args:
    sandbox_id: Unique sandbox identifier
    x_request_id: Unique request identifier for tracing (optional; server generates if omitted).

Returns:
    Response: 202 Accepted

Raises:
    HTTPException: If sandbox not found or cannot be paused

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

403.description: The authenticated user lacks permission for this operation

404.description: The requested resource does not exist

409.description: The operation conflicts with the current state

post.description:
Resume a paused sandbox.

Resumes execution of a paused sandbox.
Poll GET /sandboxes/{sandboxId} to track state transition to Running.

Args:
    sandbox_id: Unique sandbox identifier
    x_request_id: Unique request identifier for tracing (optional; server generates if omitted).

Returns:
    Response: 202 Accepted

Raises:
    HTTPException: If sandbox not found or cannot be resumed

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

403.description: The authenticated user lacks permission for this operation

404.description: The requested resource does not exist

409.description: The operation conflicts with the current state

post.description:
Renew sandbox expiration.

Renews the absolute expiration time of a sandbox.
The new expiration time must be in the future and after the current expiresAt time.

Args:
    sandbox_id: Unique sandbox identifier
    request: Renewal request with new expiration time
    x_request_id: Unique request identifier for tracing (optional; server generates if omitted).

Returns:
    RenewSandboxExpirationResponse: Updated expiration time

Raises:
    HTTPException: If sandbox not found or renewal fails

400.description: The request was invalid or malformed

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

403.description: The authenticated user lacks permission for this operation

404.description: The requested resource does not exist

409.description: The operation conflicts with the current state

get.description:
Get sandbox access endpoint.

Returns the public access endpoint URL for accessing a service running on a specific port
within the sandbox. The service must be listening on the specified port inside the sandbox
for the endpoint to be available.

Args:
    request: FastAPI request object
    sandbox_id: Unique sandbox identifier
    port: Port number where the service is listening inside the sandbox (1-65535)
    use_server_proxy: Whether to return a server-proxied URL
    x_request_id: Unique request identifier for tracing (optional; server generates if omitted).

Returns:
    Endpoint: Public endpoint URL

Raises:
    HTTPException: If sandbox not found or endpoint not available

parameters.name: use_server_proxy

schema.description: Whether to return a server-proxied URL
schema.title: Use Server Proxy

parameters.description: Whether to return a server-proxied URL

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

403.description: The authenticated user lacks permission for this operation

404.description: The requested resource does not exist

get.description: Retrieve container logs for a sandbox.

parameters.name: since

schema.description: Only return logs newer than this duration (e.g. 10m, 1h)
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: Only return logs newer than this duration (e.g. 10m, 1h)

404.description: Sandbox not found

get.description: Retrieve detailed inspection info for a sandbox container.

404.description: Sandbox not found

get.description: Retrieve events related to a sandbox.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of events to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return

404.description: Sandbox not found

get.description: One-shot diagnostics summary: inspect + events + logs.

200.description: Combined diagnostics summary as plain text

404.description: Sandbox not found

post.description:
Create a pre-warmed resource pool.

Creates a Pool CRD resource that manages a set of pre-warmed pods.
Once created, sandboxes can reference the pool via ``extensions.poolRef``
during sandbox creation to benefit from reduced cold-start latency.

Args:
    request: Pool creation request including name, pod template, and capacity spec.
    x_request_id: Optional request tracing identifier.

Returns:
    PoolResponse: The newly created pool.

400.description: The request was invalid or malformed

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

409.description: A pool with the same name already exists

501.description: Pool management is not supported in this runtime

get.description:
List all pre-warmed resource pools.

Returns all Pool resources in the configured namespace.

Args:
    x_request_id: Optional request tracing identifier.

Returns:
    ListPoolsResponse: Collection of all pools.

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

501.description: Pool management is not supported in this runtime

get.description:
Retrieve a pool by name.

Args:
    pool_name: Name of the pool to retrieve.
    x_request_id: Optional request tracing identifier.

Returns:
    PoolResponse: Current state of the pool including runtime status.

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

404.description: The requested pool does not exist

501.description: Pool management is not supported in this runtime

put.description:
Update pool capacity configuration.

Only ``capacitySpec`` (bufferMax, bufferMin, poolMax, poolMin) can be
modified after creation. To change the pod template, delete and recreate
the pool.

Args:
    pool_name: Name of the pool to update.
    request: Update request with the new capacity spec.
    x_request_id: Optional request tracing identifier.

Returns:
    PoolResponse: Updated pool state.

400.description: The request was invalid or malformed

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

404.description: The requested pool does not exist

501.description: Pool management is not supported in this runtime

delete.description:
Delete a pool.

Removes the Pool CRD resource. Pre-warmed pods managed by the pool will
be terminated by the pool controller.

Args:
    pool_name: Name of the pool to delete.
    x_request_id: Optional request tracing identifier.

Returns:
    Response: 204 No Content.

401.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

404.description: The requested pool does not exist

501.description: Pool management is not supported in this runtime

Returns:

name.title: Name
name.description: Unique name for the pool (must be a valid Kubernetes resource name).

template.title: Template
template.description: Kubernetes PodTemplateSpec defining the pod configuration for pre-warmed nodes. Follows the same schema as spec.template in a Kubernetes Deployment.

capacitySpec.description: Capacity configuration controlling pool size and buffer behavior.

CreatePoolRequest.title: CreatePoolRequest
CreatePoolRequest.description:
Request to create a new pre-warmed resource pool.

A Pool manages a set of pre-warmed pods that can be rapidly allocated
to sandboxes, reducing cold-start latency.

image.description: Container image specification for the sandbox

platform.description: Optional platform constraint for sandbox scheduling/runtime selection. If omitted, runtime default behavior applies (runtime-specific and not a fixed architecture guarantee). If specified, runtime must satisfy this platform or fail explicitly.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: Sandbox timeout in seconds (minimum 60). The maximum is controlled by server.max_sandbox_timeout_seconds. When omitted or null, the sandbox will not auto-terminate and must be deleted explicitly. Note: manual cleanup support is runtime-dependent; Kubernetes providers may reject null timeout when the workload provider does not support non-expiring sandboxes.

resourceLimits.description: Runtime resource constraints for the sandbox instance

env.title: Env
env.description: Environment variables to inject into the sandbox runtime

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Custom key-value metadata for management, filtering, and tagging

entrypoint.title: Entrypoint
entrypoint.description: The command to execute as the sandbox's entry process

networkPolicy.description: Optional outbound network policy. Shape matches the egress sidecar /policy endpoint. Empty/omitted means allow-all until updated.

secureAccess.title: Secureaccess
secureAccess.description: Opts the sandbox into secured access for endpoint access. Currently supported only for Kubernetes sandboxes exposed through ingress gateway mode. When enabled, the server provisions access credentials and returns required endpoint headers.

volumes.title: Volumes
volumes.description: Storage mounts for the sandbox. Each volume entry specifies a named backend-specific storage source and common mount settings. Exactly one backend type must be specified per volume entry.

extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.description: Opaque container for provider-specific or transient parameters not covered by the core API

CreateSandboxRequest.title: CreateSandboxRequest
CreateSandboxRequest.description: Request to create a new sandbox from a container image.

status.description: Current lifecycle status and detailed state information

platform.description: Platform constraint echoed from request or workload template. Null when no scheduling constraint is provided.

expiresAt.title: Expiresat
expiresAt.description: Timestamp when sandbox will auto-terminate. Null when manual cleanup is enabled.

entrypoint.title: Entrypoint
entrypoint.description: Entry process specification from creation request

CreateSandboxResponse.title: CreateSandboxResponse
CreateSandboxResponse.description:
Response from creating a new sandbox.

Contains essential information without image and updatedAt.

endpoint.title: Endpoint
endpoint.description: Public endpoint string (host[:port]/path) exposed for the sandbox service

headers.title: Headers
headers.description: Optional headers required when accessing the endpoint (e.g., for header-based routing).

Endpoint.title: Endpoint
Endpoint.description: Endpoint for accessing a service running in the sandbox.

code.title: Code
code.description: Machine-readable error code (e.g., INVALID_REQUEST, NOT_FOUND, INTERNAL_ERROR)

message.title: Message
message.description: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong and how to fix it

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description:
Standard error response for all non-2xx HTTP responses.

HTTP status code indicates the error category; code and message provide details.

path.title: Path
path.description: Absolute path on the host filesystem to mount.

Host.title: Host
Host.description:
Host path bind mount backend.

Maps a directory on the host filesystem into the container.
Only available when the runtime supports host mounts.

Security note: Host paths are restricted by server-side allowlist.
Users must specify paths under permitted prefixes.

ImageAuth.title: ImageAuth
ImageAuth.description: Registry authentication credentials for private container registries.

uri.title: Uri
uri.description: Container image URI in standard format (e.g., 'python:3.11', 'gcr.io/my-project/app:v1.0')

auth.description: Registry authentication credentials (required for private registries)

ImageSpec.title: ImageSpec
ImageSpec.description:
Container image specification for sandbox provisioning.

Supports public registry images and private registry images with authentication.

defaultAction.title: Defaultaction
defaultAction.description: Default action when no egress rule matches (allow | deny). If omitted, sidecar defaults to deny.

egress.title: Egress
egress.description: Ordered egress rules. Empty/omitted yields allow-all at startup.

NetworkPolicy.title: NetworkPolicy
NetworkPolicy.description: Egress network policy matching the sidecar /policy payload.

action.title: Action
action.description: Whether to allow or deny matching targets (allow | deny).

NetworkRule.title: NetworkRule
NetworkRule.description: Egress rule: allow/deny a specific domain or wildcard.

version.title: Version
version.description: ossfs major version used by runtime mount integration.

options.title: Options
options.description: Additional ossfs mount options. Runtime encodes options by version: 1.0 => 'ossfs ... -o <option>', 2.0 => 'ossfs2 config line --<option>'. Provide raw option payloads without leading '-'.

accessKeyId.title: Accesskeyid
accessKeyId.description: OSS access key ID for inline credentials mode.

accessKeySecret.title: Accesskeysecret
accessKeySecret.description: OSS access key secret for inline credentials mode.

OSSFS.title: OSSFS
OSSFS.description:
Alibaba Cloud OSS mount backend via ossfs.

The runtime mounts a host-side OSS path under ``storage.ossfs_mount_root``
and then bind-mounts the resolved path into the sandbox container. Prefix
selection is expressed via ``Volume.subPath``.
In Docker runtime, OSSFS backend requires the server host to be Linux with FUSE support.

claimName.title: Claimname
claimName.description: Name of the volume on the target platform. In Kubernetes this is the PVC name; in Docker this is the named volume name.

createIfNotExists.title: Createifnotexists
createIfNotExists.description: When true, the volume is automatically created if it does not exist. When false, referencing a non-existent volume fails with an error.

deleteOnSandboxTermination.title: Deleteonsandboxtermination
deleteOnSandboxTermination.description: When true, the volume is automatically removed when the sandbox is deleted. Only applies to volumes that were auto-created by the server (Docker only). Pre-existing volumes are never removed. Has no effect on Kubernetes PVCs, whose lifecycle is managed by the StorageClass reclaim policy.

storageClass.title: Storageclass
storageClass.description: Kubernetes StorageClass name for auto-created PVCs. None means use the cluster default. Ignored for Docker volumes.

storage.title: Storage
storage.description: Storage capacity request for auto-created PVCs (e.g. '1Gi', '10Gi'). Defaults to server-side configured value when omitted. Ignored for Docker volumes.

accessModes.title: Accessmodes
accessModes.description: Access modes for auto-created PVCs (e.g. ['ReadWriteOnce']). Defaults to ['ReadWriteOnce'] when omitted. Ignored for Docker volumes.

PVC.title: PVC
PVC.description:
Platform-managed named volume backend.

A runtime-neutral abstraction for referencing a platform-managed named volume.
If ``createIfNotExists`` is true (the default) and the volume does not
yet exist, it will be created automatically using the provisioning hints below.

- Kubernetes: maps to a PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace.
- Docker: maps to a Docker named volume (created via ``docker volume create``).

totalItems.title: Totalitems
totalItems.description: Total number of items matching the filter

hasNextPage.title: Hasnextpage
hasNextPage.description: Whether there are more pages after the current one

bufferMax.title: Buffermax
bufferMax.description: Maximum number of nodes kept in the warm buffer.

bufferMin.title: Buffermin
bufferMin.description: Minimum number of nodes that must remain in the buffer.

poolMax.title: Poolmax
poolMax.description: Maximum total number of nodes allowed in the entire pool.

poolMin.title: Poolmin
poolMin.description: Minimum total size of the pool.

PoolCapacitySpec.title: PoolCapacitySpec
PoolCapacitySpec.description: Capacity configuration that controls the size of the resource pool.

status.description: Observed runtime state of the pool. May be absent if not yet reconciled.

PoolResponse.title: PoolResponse
PoolResponse.description: Full representation of a Pool resource.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of nodes in the pool.

allocated.title: Allocated
allocated.description: Number of nodes currently allocated to sandboxes.

available.title: Available
available.description: Number of nodes currently available in the pool.

revision.title: Revision
revision.description: Latest revision identifier of the pool.

PoolStatus.title: PoolStatus
PoolStatus.description: Observed runtime state of a pool.

expiresAt.title: Expiresat
expiresAt.description: New absolute expiration time in UTC (RFC 3339 format). Must be in the future.

RenewSandboxExpirationRequest.title: RenewSandboxExpirationRequest
RenewSandboxExpirationRequest.description: Request to renew sandbox expiration time.

expiresAt.title: Expiresat
expiresAt.description: The new absolute expiration time in UTC (RFC 3339 format)

ResourceLimits.title: ResourceLimits
ResourceLimits.description:
Runtime resource constraints as key-value pairs.

Similar to Kubernetes resource specifications, allows flexible definition
of resource limits. Common resource types include cpu, memory, and gpu.

image.description: Container image specification used to provision this sandbox

platform.description: Platform constraint echoed from request or workload template. Null when no scheduling constraint is provided.

status.description: Current lifecycle status and detailed state information

entrypoint.title: Entrypoint
entrypoint.description: The command to execute as the sandbox's entry process

expiresAt.title: Expiresat
expiresAt.description: Timestamp when sandbox will auto-terminate. Null when manual cleanup is enabled.

Sandbox.title: Sandbox
Sandbox.description:
Runtime execution environment provisioned from a container image.

This is the complete representation of the sandbox resource.

state.title: State
state.description: Current lifecycle state (Pending, Running, Pausing, Paused, Stopping, Terminated, Failed)

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Short machine-readable reason code for the current state

message.title: Message
message.description: Human-readable message describing the current state or reason for state transition

lastTransitionAt.title: Lasttransitionat
lastTransitionAt.description: Timestamp of the last state transition

SandboxStatus.title: SandboxStatus
SandboxStatus.description: Detailed status information with lifecycle state and transition details.

capacitySpec.description: New capacity configuration for the pool.

UpdatePoolRequest.title: UpdatePoolRequest
UpdatePoolRequest.description:
Request to update an existing pool's capacity configuration.

Only capacity settings can be updated after pool creation.
Updating the pod template requires recreating the pool.

name.title: Name
name.description: Unique identifier for the volume within the sandbox.

pvc.description: Platform-managed named volume backend (PVC in Kubernetes, named volume in Docker).

mountPath.title: Mountpath
mountPath.description: Absolute path inside the container where the volume is mounted.

readOnly.title: Readonly
readOnly.description: If true, the volume is mounted as read-only. Defaults to false (read-write).

subPath.title: Subpath
subPath.description: Optional subdirectory under the backend path to mount.

Volume.title: Volume
Volume.description:
Storage mount definition for a sandbox.

Each volume entry contains:
- A unique name identifier
- Exactly one backend struct (host, pvc, etc.) with backend-specific fields
- Common mount settings (mountPath, readOnly, subPath)
